exercises.title: LWE Problem Demonstration
exercises.description: Implement a simple demonstration of the LWE problem.
tasks: Generate a random secret vector s of dimension n=4
Create 10 LWE samples (a_i, b_i) where b_i = <a_i, s> + e_i mod q
Demonstrate that without knowing the errors, recovering s is hard
Show that with known errors, standard linear algebra recovers s
Explain why this illustrates the LWE hardness
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def generate_lwe_samples(s, n, q, num_samples):
    """Generate LWE samples given secret s."""
    samples = []
    for _ in range(num_samples):
        a = np.random.randint(0, q, n)
        e = np.random.randint(-2, 3)  # Small error
        b = (np.dot(a, s) + e) % q
        samples.append((a, b, e))
    return samples

# Your implementation here
exercises.expectedOutput: Code demonstrating LWE with and without error recovery
hints: Use small parameters (n=4, q=23) for demonstration
Small errors make least-squares solutions fail

exercises.title: Polynomial Ring Arithmetic
exercises.description: Implement basic polynomial ring operations used in ML-KEM.
tasks: Implement polynomial addition in Zq[X]/(X^n + 1)
Implement polynomial multiplication in Zq[X]/(X^n + 1)
Demonstrate how X^n ≡ -1 (mod X^n + 1) affects multiplication
Compare naive multiplication vs NTT-based multiplication (conceptually)
Verify your implementation with test cases
hints: For X^n + 1, terms with degree >= n wrap with negation

exercises.title: Security Level Comparison
exercises.description: Research and compare the security of LWE, Ring-LWE, and Module-LWE.
tasks: Describe the structural differences between the three variants
Research known attacks on each variant
Explain why Module-LWE was chosen for ML-KEM
Discuss the security/efficiency tradeoff
Assess whether Ring-LWE attacks apply to Module-LWE
exercises.expectedOutput: A research document comparing LWE variants with security analysis
hints: Ring-LWE has more algebraic structure than Module-LWE
Look for papers on 'algebraic attacks' on Ring-LWE
Module-LWE with small k approaches Ring-LWE, large k approaches LWE

exercises.title: NTT Performance Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze the performance impact of NTT on ML-KEM operations.
tasks: Calculate the number of operations for naive polynomial multiplication
Calculate the number of operations using NTT
Compute the speedup for n=256
Explain why ML-KEM stores polynomials in NTT form
Identify which operations require NTT and inverse NTT
exercises.expectedOutput: A performance analysis document with calculations
hints: Naive: O(n²), NTT: O(n log n)
Key generation, encapsulation, and decapsulation all use NTT
